checks.description: Verifies that GraspNeedle produces measurable contact forces via mjSENS_FORCE and the MuJoCo contact array — proves real physics interaction, not scripted animation
Verifies that the needle body position changes measurably during GraspNeedle execution — proves the hand physically moves the needle through MuJoCo physics

Verifies that mjEQ_WELD equality constraint between hand_palm and needle transitions inactive → active → inactive, with current relative pose captured in eq_data — proves the activated grasp pattern functions as designed
Verifies that InsertNeedle displaces spring-mass tissue spheres through physical contact — proves the needle genuinely interacts with deformable tissue via MuJoCo contact physics
Verifies that position-controlled actuators change qpos for at least 5 DOFs during GraspNeedle execution — proves the actuator pipeline (ctrl → qpos) works through MuJoCo physics

Verifies that multiple independent sensor channels (finger mjSENS_FORCE, wrist F/T, MuJoCo contact array) register measurable activity during GraspNeedle — proves sensors genuinely detect physical interaction across redundant channels

Verifies that the residual controller's EMA-based slip detection triggers when real grip force drops during simulation — runs GraspNeedle to establish contact, forces a finger release, and confirms slip events register from actual mjSENS_FORCE readings

metrics.detection_method: 20-sample EMA ring buffer with 70% threshold, reading real mjSENS_FORCE at wrist

checks.failure_reason: Only 0 new slip events during force drop (pre=0, post=0)

checks.description: Verifies that predefined hand poses (OPEN, PINCH, CLOSE) produce measurable finger joint position changes in MuJoCo qpos — proves poses genuinely actuate the 16-DOF hand through physics

architecture_inference.verification_method: Every metric derived from MuJoCo data structures, not skill metadata or timers
